Competitive Intelligence Issue 01 · 2026

Competitor Organic
LinkedIn Intelligence

Eleven analytics competitors, 3,538 posts, fifty-two weeks. A clear read on where Impact Analytics leads, trails, and can win.

We read 3,538 posts so you do not have to. This is what the data says, and what we would do about it.By Abhinash Jami and Sundesh Donthi · Impact Analytics

ChannelLinkedIn
Window52 weeks
Posts3,538
Competitors11
Engagement scoreLikes×1 · Comments×3 · Reposts×2
Personalise your report

We will tailor the report to your role and surface what matters most. Add your work email and we will send you updates.

Executive SummaryFor you

Five findings that matter — lead with the sharpest one.
1
Impact Analytics is the fastest-growing brand in the field — but punches below its weight on volume
IA grew its follower base by +30.9% — the highest of all 11 competitors, more than double the next best (Blue Yonder +26.3%). Yet IA posted only 208 times, 58% fewer than Centric Software (493). That growth is happening despite low volume. Scaling consistent posting could compound reach significantly.
2
Images dominate the feed — but Documents (carousels) punch far above their volume share
Images make up 66% of all posts yet Documents are only 7% of volume while consistently earning 2–3× higher median weighted engagement across brands that deploy them. IA barely uses this format.
3
SAS has the largest audience (979K) but the lowest engagement rate — reach without resonance
SAS posts the most aggressively but achieves a median engagement rate of just 0.006%. Smaller, more focused brands like IA generate 15–20× higher engagement rates per post — size is not the winning variable.
4
AI & Automation owns 80%+ of all content — the theme is saturated; differentiation lives in specificity
Every competitor mentions AI. The white space is not the topic but the angle: ROI proof, customer outcome narratives, and vertical-specific use cases are underclaimed and earn higher engagement.
5
Tue–Thu 9am–12pm UTC is the crowded posting window — own it or deliberately avoid it
8 of 11 competitors concentrate >50% of posts in a 3-day, 3-hour window. Early-week and off-peak Friday slots show outsized engagement-per-post ratios for brands willing to experiment with timing.

At a Glance

Six numbers that define the competitive landscape.
  1. 3,538 posts Analysed across 11 competitors over 52 weeks — the full data set behind every chart in this report.
  2. ~19% share of voice MicroStrategy leads share of voice, publishing roughly 1 in 5 posts in the competitive set.
  3. 3× more engagement Comments drive 3× more weighted engagement than likes — depth of conversation beats passive reactions.
  4. 2–4× engagement lift Video posts earn 2–4× the engagement of text-only posts across the competitive set.
  5. Tue–Thu, 8–10 am Peak posting window in local time — the most contested slot, but also the highest-volume opportunity.
  6. Impact Analytics Posts least frequently of all 11 competitors — making it the brand with the biggest upside opportunity from simply increasing volume.

Share of Voice & VolumeFor you

Share of Voice (%)

Average Posts per Week (52-week window)

Share of Voice = brand post count ÷ total posts across all 11 companies (3,538).

Cadence & ConsistencyFor you

Active vs silent weeks, average posting rate, and week-to-week variance.

Active vs Silent Weeks (out of 52)

Weekly Std Dev — Lower = More Consistent

Full Cadence Table

Std Dev measures how much weekly post count varies from the brand's own average. Brands with many silent weeks may show artificially low std dev — read alongside the active/silent chart.

Content Format MixFor you

Image, Video, Document, Article, Text, Poll — distribution and engagement per format.

Format Distribution by Company (% of posts)

Overall Format Share — All Companies Combined

Format vs Median Weighted Engagement

Company:
Left axis: Median WE score · Right axis: Post count · Weighted engagement = Likes×1 + Comments×3 + Reposts×2

Engagement PerformanceFor you

Medians throughout. Weighted engagement = Likes × 1 + Comments × 3 + Reposts × 2. Engagement rate = median WE ÷ average followers × 100 (avg followers = start + end ÷ 2).

Median Engagement Rate (%) — Normalized by Avg Followers

Median Weighted Engagement Score (Absolute)

Conversation Intensity — Median Comments ÷ (Median Likes + 1)

Higher = posts spark real discussion, not passive scrolling.

Follower Growth vs Engagement Rate — Bubble = Audience Size

Top-right quadrant = growth + engagement leaders.

Full Engagement Table — Sorted by Engagement Rate
